node.statement: (Face Dimension Gap and Automatic Purity). Let A be a unital *-algebra. Define the face dimension gap of S(A) as the set G(A) = {d ∈ N_{>0} : no face of S(A) has dimension d}. If G(A) ⊇ {1, 2, ..., ℓ-1} (i.e., the smallest positive face dimension is ≥ ℓ), then for any C = {φ ∈ S(A) : φ(a_j) = β_j, j=1,...,m} with m ≤ ℓ-1, every extreme point of C is automatically a pure state (extreme in S(A) itself). For B(H): G = {1,2}, so m ≤ 2 constraints suffice for automatic purity.
